data.title: update-artifacts migration phase tags PIL-bearing open epics (idempotent)
data.description:
As a host project maintainer running `dso update-artifacts`, I want pre-existing brainstormed epics automatically tagged so that my sprint list does not suddenly hide all my decomposed work after the filter swap.

## Done Definition
- update-artifacts.sh contains a new named migration phase invoked by the existing `dso update-artifacts` shim
- Phase self-guards against plugin-source-repo execution (no-op exit 0 with a logged notice)
- Phase checks for marker file `.claude/.brainstorm-tag-migration-v1`; if present, no-op exit 0 immediately
- Phase iterates open epics via the ticket reducer (not raw directory scan)
- For each open epic: greps description AND comment event bodies for the `### Planning Intelligence Log` heading
- On match: performs read-merge-write to add `brainstorm:complete` (only if not already tagged; skips redundant EDIT events)
- On no match: prints `UNMATCHED: <epic-id>` to stdout
- After processing, writes the marker file (`.claude/.brainstorm-tag-migration-v1`)
- Exit code is 0 on successful completion regardless of unmatched count
- Re-run produces identical tag state and identical UNMATCHED stdout; second run exits 0 immediately via marker check
- Fixture test: 2 epics with PIL (1 in description, 1 in comment), 1 epic without PIL; migration tags the 2 + emits 1 UNMATCHED line; second run exits 0 with no tracker changes

## Considerations
- [Performance] Iterates all open epics + greps event files; consider timeout-friendly implementation for trackers with 100+ epics
- [Reliability] Per-epic tag-state verification via reducer protects against double-edits on partial-run recovery
- [Maintainability] Share read-merge-write tag helper with Story ce5f-9c4e if reasonable (bash function or Python helper in plugins/dso/scripts/)
- [Testing] Fixture needs both description-PIL and comment-PIL cases; also a plugin-source-repo guard case

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked.
